title: BGP EVPN Route Withdrawal Rate and Flap Storms
exclusions: VXLAN **VTEP** reachability and tunnel convergence narratives without withdrawal-velocity math (see UC-18.3.4); hardware forwarding shelf occupancy without BGP UPDATE semantics (see UC-18.3.20); cabling and BGP-neighbor existence posture without NLRI churn rates (see UC-18.3.14); Kubernetes CNI policy churn on hosts (see UC-18.3.1); SDN controller quorum drama without leaf BGP peers (see UC-18.3.3); CI/CD manifest audit trails lacking BGP syslog (see UC-18.3.8); certification lab firmware banners alone without route telemetry (see UC-18.3.13).
dataSources: `index=dc_fabric` / `index=network_ops` / `index=cisco_dc` / `index=os` / `index=network` combining **`sourcetype=cisco:nxos:bgp`** (**`BGP-5-ADJCHANGE`**, **`BGP-5-WITHDRAW_BATCH`**, **`BGP-3-RT_FILTER_ERROR`**, **`BGP-5-WITHDRAW`**), **`sourcetype=cisco:nxos:syslog`** (BGP mnemonics interleaved), **`sourcetype=arista:eos:bgp_evpn`** (**`BGP-5-NOTIFICATION-RECEIVED`**, **`BGP-3-PREFIX-CHANGE`**), **`sourcetype=juniper:junos:bgp`** (**`RPD_BGP_NEIGHBOR_STATE_CHANGED`**, **`RPD_PREFIX_THRESHOLD_EXCEEDED`**), **`sourcetype=sonic:show:bgp_evpn`** (FRR **`bgpd`** **`show bgp l2vpn evpn statistics`** JSON via **`vtysh`**), **`sourcetype=cumulus:netq:bgp_evpn`** (NetQ agent gRPC), and aggregator **`sourcetype=bgp:evpn:withdraw_event`** built from scripted **`show bgp l2vpn evpn statistics neighbor`** polls stamping **`wdr_recv_count`** / **`wdr_sent_count`** counters aligned per **`peer_ip`**. Fields normalized: **`local_host`**, **`peer_ip`**, **`peer_asn`**, **`route_type`**, **`prefix`**, **`wdr_count`**, **`event_time`**, optional **`route_target_changed`**, **`route_refresh_event`**, **`ecmp_paths_before`** / **`ecmp_paths_after`** when telemetry exposes hash breadth.
app: **Splunk Add-on for Cisco IOS/NX-OS** ([Splunkbase **2846**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2846)); **Splunk Add-on for Arista EOS** ([Splunkbase **4495**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4495)); **Splunk Add-on for Juniper Networks** ([Splunkbase **2847**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2847)); optional **Splunk Connect for Syslog** for scaled syslog fan-in; bespoke modular inputs issuing NX-OS **`show bgp l2vpn evpn statistics`**, **`show bgp l2vpn evpn ipv4-mvpn neighbor`** received-route summaries, EOS **`show bgp evpn statistics`**, Junos **`show bgp neighbor`** against **`table evpn.0`**, SONiC **`vtysh`** JSON snapshots landing **`sonic:show:bgp_evpn`** rows.
spl:
(index=dc_fabric OR index=network_ops OR index=cisco_dc OR index=os OR index=network)
(sourcetype="cisco:nxos:bgp" OR sourcetype="cisco:nxos:syslog" OR sourcetype="arista:eos:bgp_evpn" OR sourcetype="juniper:junos:bgp" OR sourcetype="sonic:show:bgp_evpn" OR sourcetype="cumulus:netq:bgp_evpn" OR sourcetype="bgp:evpn:withdraw_event")
earliest=-4h@m
| eval local_host=trim(coalesce(local_host, hostname, switch_name, device_name, host, ""))
| eval peer_ip=trim(coalesce(peer_ip, neighbor_ip, bgp_neighbor, neighbor_address, peer_address, remote_peer, ""))
| eval peer_asn=trim(coalesce(peer_asn, remote_as, neighbor_asn, asn_remote, ""))
| eval route_type=trim(coalesce(route_type, evpn_nlri_type, nlri_route_type, bgp_evpn_rt, ""))
| eval event_time=coalesce(event_time, _time)
| eval wdr_count=tonumber(coalesce(wdr_count, withdrawn_updates, withdraw_batch_count, withdraw_events, prefix_withdraw_cnt, wdr_recv_delta, ""))
| eval rt_upper=upper(route_type)
| eval route_bucket=case(
    match(rt_upper,"(?i)TYPE.?1|^1$|EAD|AUTO.?DISCOVERY"), "type1_ead",
    match(rt_upper,"(?i)TYPE.?2|^2$|MAC.?IP"), "type2_mac_ip",
    match(rt_upper,"(?i)TYPE.?3|^3$|IMET|INCLUSIVE"), "type3_imet",
    match(rt_upper,"(?i)TYPE.?4|^4$|ESI|SEGMENT"), "type4_esi",
    match(rt_upper,"(?i)TYPE.?5|^5$|PREFIX|IP.?PREFIX"), "type5_prefix",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)BGP-5-WITHDRAW|WITHDRAW_BATCH|wdr_recv"), "withdraw_sig_only",
    "type_unspecified")
| eval withdraw_hit=if(
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)withdraw|withdrawn|wdr_recv|revoked") OR (isnotnull(wdr_count) AND wdr_count>0) OR lower(update_action)="withdraw",
    1, 0)
| where withdraw_hit=1
| eval vol=if(isnotnull(wdr_count) AND wdr_count>0, wdr_count, 1)
| bin _time span=1m
| stats sum(vol) AS withdrawn_volume,
        latest(peer_asn) AS peer_asn_seen,
        values(prefix) AS sample_prefix,
    BY _time local_host peer_ip route_bucket
| eval type2_withdrawals_per_min=if(route_bucket=="type2_mac_ip", withdrawn_volume, null())
| eval type5_withdrawals_per_min=if(route_bucket=="type5_prefix", withdrawn_volume, null())
| eval type3_withdrawals_per_min=if(route_bucket=="type3_imet", withdrawn_volume, null())

| streamstats window=30 global=f avg(withdrawn_volume) AS baseline_avg BY local_host peer_ip route_bucket
| eval storm_ratio=if(baseline_avg>0 AND withdrawn_volume>(baseline_avg*3), withdrawn_volume/baseline_avg, null())
| eval storm_flag=case(
    route_bucket=="type5_prefix" AND withdrawn_volume>50, "type5_storm",
    route_bucket=="type3_imet" AND withdrawn_volume>5, "type3_anomaly",
    route_bucket=="type2_mac_ip" AND withdrawn_volume>1000 AND isnotnull(storm_ratio), "type2_velocity_burst",
    route_bucket=="type2_mac_ip" AND withdrawn_volume>100 AND withdrawn_volume>(baseline_avg*3), "type2_sigma_burst",
    route_bucket IN ("type4_esi","type1_ead") AND withdrawn_volume>100, "esi_segment_burst",
    null())
| where isnotnull(storm_flag)
| eval ecmp_paths_before=tonumber(ecmp_paths_before)
| eval ecmp_paths_after=tonumber(ecmp_paths_after)
| eval ecmp_shrink_pct=if(isnotnull(ecmp_paths_before) AND ecmp_paths_before>0 AND isnotnull(ecmp_paths_after), round(100*(ecmp_paths_before-ecmp_paths_after)/ecmp_paths_before, 2), null())
| sort - withdrawn_volume
| head 400
| table _time event_time local_host peer_ip peer_asn_seen route_bucket withdrawn_volume baseline_avg storm_ratio storm_flag type2_withdrawals_per_min type5_withdrawals_per_min type3_withdrawals_per_min sample_prefix ecmp_shrink_pct
description:
BGP EVPN fabrics propagate overlay reachability through RFC 7432 and RFC 8365 NLRI families spanning Type-1 Ethernet Auto-Discovery, Type-2 MAC/IP advertisements, Type-3 inclusive multicast Ethernet-tag routes, Type-4 Ethernet-segment selection, and Type-5 IP-prefix overlays. Withdrawals—not merely advertisements—drive how fast speakers retract paths from RIB, ripple updates toward remote peers, and shrink dataplane ECMP breadth when redundant next hops evaporate in one blast. When **withdrawn** UPDATE volume spikes per peer within a minute bucket, convergence stretches beyond steady MAC-learning timers: downstream TOR switches purge Hardware bridging rows, spine route-reflector mirrors fan withdrawals outward, and operators observe lengthened convergence tails measured peer-to-peer rather than solely hop-count estimates.

Large bursts strain controller-plane CPUs still honoring UPDATE pacing, reorder BGP scanner queues, and interact badly with telemetry collectors polling **evpn statistics** concurrently—creating observer-effect lag where Splunk timestamps trail CLI counters briefly yet storm signatures persist across sequential buckets. Withdrawal storms differ materially from benign mobility churn because paired Type-2 advertisements plus withdrawals cancel statistically during host moves; storms skew toward unmatched withdrawal dominance sourced from one **peer_ip**, aligning with leaf reload fingerprints, Ethernet-segment flap narratives tying Type-1 plus Type-4 retractions together, or route-target policy edits masquerading as dataplane faults unless annotated explicitly.
value:
Splunk velocity math exposes storm fingerprints early—minutes ahead of synthetic probes noticing ECMP imbalance—by aligning normalized **`withdrawn`** counters per **`route_bucket`**, **`peer_asn`**, and **`local_host`**. Teams gain predictive cues when **`storm_ratio`** clears three-sigma style multiples against rolling **`baseline_avg`** slices per peer family: Type-5 storms exceeding fifty withdrawals per minute flag stretched-L3 churn isolating perimeter mistakes from accidental spine resets; Type-3 thresholds north of five per minute spotlight multicast replication rebuilds deserving correlation against operational toggles; paired **`type2_withdrawals_per_min`** alongside advertisement counters distinguishes mobility symmetry from asymmetric teardown indicative of remote **VTEP** departure.

Operational outcomes include prioritized escalation keyed by **`storm_flag`** taxonomy (**`type5_storm`**, **`esi_segment_burst`**, **`type2_sigma_burst`**), **`ECMP set shrinkage`** forecasting via **`ecmp_shrink_pct`** when collectors expose hash-path cardinality deltas, forensic grouping using **`sample_prefix`** clusters lifted from syslog phrases or BMP-derived **`prefix`** lists, and convergence-extension narratives anchored on sequential **`event_time`** stamps correlated against UC-18.3.4 overlay timelines plus UC-18.3.14 topology posture—closing loops between control-plane churn and silent east-west degradation without asking operators to babysit vendor CLIs continuously.
implementation: Deploy Splunkbase **2846**, **4495**, **2847** parsers on Heavy Forwarders; stage scripted **`show bgp l2vpn evpn statistics`** polls per spine-leaf peer list every **60s** into **`bgp:evpn:withdraw_event`** with **`FIELDALIAS`** mapping **`wdr_recv_count`** deltas into **`wdr_count`**; normalize syslog **`withdraw_hit`** signatures across **`cisco:nxos:bgp`**, **`arista:eos:bgp_evpn`**, **`juniper:junos:bgp`**, **`sonic:show:bgp_evpn`**, **`cumulus:netq:bgp_evpn`**; publish macro **`evpn_withdraw_velocity_core`** referencing governance-approved index unions.
visualization: (1) **Heat-map** — **`peer_ip`** × **`route_bucket`** colored by **`withdrawn_volume`** per minute. (2) **Timechart** — **`type2_withdrawals_per_min`** stacked alongside **`type5_withdrawals_per_min`**. (3) **Gauge row** — **`ecmp_shrink_pct`** worst offenders with **`storm_flag`** overlays. (4) **Table** — top storms listing **`local_host`**, **`peer_asn_seen`**, **`storm_ratio`**, **`sample_prefix`**. (5) **Timeline** — sequential **`event_time`** markers paired with Cisco **NDI** anomaly ribbons when **`cisco:ndi:*`** lanes exist.
schema: CIM: **Network_Traffic** (BGP UPDATE narratives mapped into routing-performance proxies, **`withdrawn`** telemetry mirroring control-plane instability markers) plus **Alerts** ( **`storm_flag`** severity taxonomy emitted via correlation searches feeding **`alert`** **`severity`** attributes where routing SMEs approve Splunk workflows).
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate **Alerts** summaries when **`storm_flag`** correlation searches land indexed alert artifacts nightly; retain raw **`Network_Traffic`** lanes without premature acceleration because heterogeneous syslog mixes seldom align with singular **`perf`** metric extracts.

references.title: IETF RFC 7432 — BGP MPLS-Based Ethernet VPN
IETF RFC 8365 — Network Virtualization Overlay Solution Using BGP EVPN
Cisco Nexus 9000 Series NX-OS BGP EVPN Configuration Guide

knownFalsePositives:
**firmware_upgrade_window** — Orchestrated leaf reloads during supervised firmware pushes deliberately tear down BGP-EVPN tables for roughly thirty-to-sixty seconds while images commit—withdrawal counters leap upward yet operators already expect that choreography—suppress **`storm_flag`** hits whenever CMDB tags **`firmware_upgrade_window`** pair **`local_host`** identities with CAB-approved **`ISSU`** sequencing backed by reload tickets tied to those hosts.

**gr_helper_marker_walkthrough** — Peers honoring **`RFC`** **`4724`** **`Restart`** **`helper`** semantics retain **`STALE`** markings while **`WITHDRAWN`** tallies oscillate apart from instantaneous dataplane outages—cross-check syslog **`Neighbor`** banners citing **`Graceful`** timers alongside **`Established`** uptime versus **`withdraw_hit`** timelines before blaming **`esi_segment_burst`** fingerprints alone.

**mso_route_target_changeover** — Multi-site orchestrator pushes sometimes revise **`route-target`** import/export tuples—routes briefly **`withdrawn`** then readvertised resembling storms—honor **`route_target_changed`** booleans inside **`bgp:evpn:withdraw_event`** aggregates plus **`BGP-3-RT_FILTER_ERROR`** narratives before escalating **`type5_storm`** outcomes tied to unrelated WAN faults.

**imet_multicast_tree_rebuild** — Operators flipping **BUM** replication (**HEAD**, **MTEP**, **SOURCE**) modes or adjusting overlay **MTU** knobs rebuild inclusive multicast Ethernet-tag trees—**Type-3** withdrawals spike benignly—tie positives to **`MaintenanceWindow`** tickets citing **`imet_multicast_tree_rebuild`** chores rather than paging multicast cores absent corroborating optics alarms.

**route_refresh_event** — Outbound **`route-map`** edits routinely solicit **`route-refresh`** exchanges—both peers **`withdraw`** then re-announce prefixes symmetrically—carry **`route_refresh_event`** markers harvested from syslog (**`BGP-5-ROUTEREFRESH`** style signatures) alongside **`CHG`** identifiers describing policy edits so **`withdrawn_volume`** surges downgrade automatically.

**mac_aging_time_reconfig** — Operators shortening MAC aging timers provoke mass **`Type-2`** withdrawals followed by fresh advertisements while tables stabilize—annotate **`mac_aging_time_reconfig`** maintenance tickets suppressing pager noise distinct from stealth evacuations lacking paired ticketing clues.

**evpn_arp_routing_toggle** — Feature toggles enabling or disabling **`evpn-arp-routing`** reshape host-route advertisement behavior—large one-time **`withdraw`** bursts precede steady counters afterward—suppress until **`baseline_avg`** recomputes post-change following automation logs documenting **`evpn_arp_routing_toggle`** intent.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Splunk TA footprint — Deploy Splunk Add-on for Cisco IOS/NX-OS (Splunkbase 2846), Splunk Add-on for Arista EOS (Splunkbase 4495), and Splunk Add-on for Juniper (Splunkbase 2847) on Heavy Forwarders plus parsing Indexers so `cisco:nxos:bgp`, `cisco:nxos:syslog`, `arista:eos:bgp_evpn`, `juniper:junos:bgp` honor syslog PRI fidelity.
• Scripted telemetry — Provision modular inputs or Ansible batches issuing `show bgp l2vpn evpn statistics` per `peer_ip` on 60 second cadences across Nexus 9000, `show bgp evpn statistics` on Arista 7280, `show bgp neighbor` referencing `table evpn.0` on QFX5120, `vtysh -c "show bgp l2vpn evpn statistics json"` on SONiC Dell Z9332, `cumulus:netq:bgp_evpn` gRPC lanes on Mellanox SN3700-class switches.
• Aggregator discipline — Normalize `bgp:evpn:withdraw_event` sourcetype emitting `local_host`, `peer_ip`, `peer_asn`, `route_type`, `prefix`, `wdr_count`, `event_time`, optional `route_target_changed`, `route_refresh_event`, `ecmp_paths_before`/`ecmp_paths_after` deltas sourced from BMP exports when licensed.
• Roles — Grant `network_observer` indexes `dc_fabric`, `network_ops`, `cisco_dc`, `network`, `os` per governance naming.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Cisco Nexus 9000 — Ensure `BGP-5-WITHDRAW`, `BGP-5-WITHDRAW_BATCH`, `BGP-3-RT_FILTER_ERROR`, `BGP-5-ADJCHANGE` mnemonics land `cisco:nxos:bgp` while `BGP-5-WITHDRAW` strings inside `cisco:nxos:syslog` remain searchable—stamp `FIELDALIAS` maps bridging `wdr_recv_count` counters parsed from `show bgp l2vpn evpn statistics neighbor` transcripts into `wdr_count` increments minute-over-minute.
(2) Arista EOS — Capture `BGP-5-NOTIFICATION-RECEIVED` and `BGP-3-PREFIX-CHANGE` narratives inside `arista:eos:bgp_evpn`, aligning `withdraw_hit` regex families with `withdrawn` verbs emitted via `show bgp evpn statistics` JSON dumps duplicated through HEC when `eAPI` quotas permit.
(3) Juniper Junos — Combine `RPD_BGP_NEIGHBOR_STATE_CHANGED` plus `RPD_PREFIX_THRESHOLD_EXCEEDED` syslog cues inside `juniper:junos:bgp` alongside scripted `received-routes` summaries referencing `evpn.0` tables—preserve `route_bucket` fidelity between syslog storms versus polled `withdrawn` counters.
(4) SONiC / FRRouting — Serialize `sonic:show:bgp_evpn` JSON snapshots capturing `withdrawn` tallies without stripping `peer_ip` masks—confirm `syncd` identities align `host` fields expected downstream.
(5) Cumulus NetQ — Subscribe `cumulus:netq:bgp_evpn` feeds verifying ASN `peer_asn` parity versus CMDB `expected_bgp_peers` lookups borrowed from UC-18.3.14 guardrails.
(6) Custom aggregator — Fuse scripted deltas plus syslog hits into `bgp:evpn:withdraw_event` so `storm_flag` SPL consumes unified `withdrawn_volume` math irrespective of ingestion lane—embed `suppress` macros honoring `firmware_upgrade_window`, `gr_helper_marker_walkthrough`, `mso_route_target_changeover` toggles emitted through lookups.

Step 2 — Splunk SPL search and alert configuration
Install saved search `evpn_withdraw_velocity_core` referencing the fenced SPL below with five-minute cron **`*/5` plus `earliest=-4h@m` investigative defaults—wrap `route_bucket` thresholds inside macros `withdraw_threshold_type5`, `withdraw_threshold_type3`, `withdraw_sigma_multiplier` defaulting multiplier `3` aligning `baseline_avg` bursts.
Throttle paging `peer_ip` plus `route_bucket` pairs for `30` minutes unless `withdrawn_volume` doubles—prevent duplicate `storm_flag` tickets whenever `streamstats` windows overlap transient syslog bursts.
Annotate alerts with `sample_prefix` tails plus `peer_asn_seen` enabling downstream traceroute drills toward offending `VTEP`** attachments.
Escalate **`type5_storm`** rows faster than **`type2_sigma_burst`** because **`Type-5`** volumes remain comparatively tiny—protect **`imet_multicast_tree_rebuild`** contexts via **`MaintenanceWindow`** overlays referencing **`CHANGE`** identifiers.

```spl
(index=dc_fabric OR index=network_ops OR index=cisco_dc OR index=os OR index=network)
(sourcetype="cisco:nxos:bgp" OR sourcetype="cisco:nxos:syslog" OR sourcetype="arista:eos:bgp_evpn" OR sourcetype="juniper:junos:bgp" OR sourcetype="sonic:show:bgp_evpn" OR sourcetype="cumulus:netq:bgp_evpn" OR sourcetype="bgp:evpn:withdraw_event")
earliest=-4h@m
| eval local_host=trim(coalesce(local_host, hostname, switch_name, device_name, host, ""))
| eval peer_ip=trim(coalesce(peer_ip, neighbor_ip, bgp_neighbor, neighbor_address, peer_address, remote_peer, ""))
| eval peer_asn=trim(coalesce(peer_asn, remote_as, neighbor_asn, asn_remote, ""))
| eval route_type=trim(coalesce(route_type, evpn_nlri_type, nlri_route_type, bgp_evpn_rt, ""))
| eval event_time=coalesce(event_time, _time)
| eval wdr_count=tonumber(coalesce(wdr_count, withdrawn_updates, withdraw_batch_count, withdraw_events, prefix_withdraw_cnt, wdr_recv_delta, ""))
| eval rt_upper=upper(route_type)
| eval route_bucket=case(
    match(rt_upper,"(?i)TYPE.?1|^1$|EAD|AUTO.?DISCOVERY"), "type1_ead",
    match(rt_upper,"(?i)TYPE.?2|^2$|MAC.?IP"), "type2_mac_ip",
    match(rt_upper,"(?i)TYPE.?3|^3$|IMET|INCLUSIVE"), "type3_imet",
    match(rt_upper,"(?i)TYPE.?4|^4$|ESI|SEGMENT"), "type4_esi",
    match(rt_upper,"(?i)TYPE.?5|^5$|PREFIX|IP.?PREFIX"), "type5_prefix",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)BGP-5-WITHDRAW|WITHDRAW_BATCH|wdr_recv"), "withdraw_sig_only",
    "type_unspecified")
| eval withdraw_hit=if(
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)withdraw|withdrawn|wdr_recv|revoked") OR (isnotnull(wdr_count) AND wdr_count>0) OR lower(update_action)="withdraw",
    1, 0)
| where withdraw_hit=1
| eval vol=if(isnotnull(wdr_count) AND wdr_count>0, wdr_count, 1)
| bin _time span=1m
| stats sum(vol) AS withdrawn_volume,
        latest(peer_asn) AS peer_asn_seen,
        values(prefix) AS sample_prefix,
        dc(route_bucket) AS route_family_mix
    BY _time local_host peer_ip route_bucket
| eval type2_withdrawals_per_min=if(route_bucket=="type2_mac_ip", withdrawn_volume, null())
| eval type5_withdrawals_per_min=if(route_bucket=="type5_prefix", withdrawn_volume, null())
| eval type3_withdrawals_per_min=if(route_bucket=="type3_imet", withdrawn_volume, null())
| streamstats window=30 global=f avg(withdrawn_volume) AS baseline_avg BY local_host peer_ip route_bucket
| eval storm_ratio=if(baseline_avg>0 AND withdrawn_volume>(baseline_avg*3), withdrawn_volume/baseline_avg, null())
| eval storm_flag=case(
    route_bucket=="type5_prefix" AND withdrawn_volume>50, "type5_storm",
    route_bucket=="type3_imet" AND withdrawn_volume>5, "type3_anomaly",
    route_bucket=="type2_mac_ip" AND withdrawn_volume>1000 AND isnotnull(storm_ratio), "type2_velocity_burst",
    route_bucket=="type2_mac_ip" AND withdrawn_volume>100 AND withdrawn_volume>(baseline_avg*3), "type2_sigma_burst",
    route_bucket IN ("type4_esi","type1_ead") AND withdrawn_volume>100, "esi_segment_burst",
    null())
| where isnotnull(storm_flag)
| eval ecmp_paths_before=tonumber(ecmp_paths_before)
| eval ecmp_paths_after=tonumber(ecmp_paths_after)
| eval ecmp_shrink_pct=if(isnotnull(ecmp_paths_before) AND ecmp_paths_before>0 AND isnotnull(ecmp_paths_after), round(100*(ecmp_paths_before-ecmp_paths_after)/ecmp_paths_before, 2), null())
| sort - withdrawn_volume
| head 400
| table _time event_time local_host peer_ip peer_asn_seen route_bucket withdrawn_volume baseline_avg storm_ratio storm_flag type2_withdrawals_per_min type5_withdrawals_per_min type3_withdrawals_per_min route_family_mix sample_prefix ecmp_shrink_pct
```

Correlation narrative — **`storm_ratio`** exposes **`withdrawn_volume`** multiples exceeding **`baseline_avg`** smoothing—**`route_bucket`** thresholds differentiate **`type5_storm`** severity versus **`type2_sigma_burst`** churn.

Step 3 — Validate
SSH parity drills comparing Splunk **`withdrawn_volume`** peaks versus **`show bgp l2vpn evpn statistics`** (**`wdr_recv_count`**/**`wdr_sent_count`**) counters on Nexus 9332C leaves—EOS **`show bgp evpn statistics`** mirrors **`peer_ip`** aggregates—Junos **`show bgp neighbor`** **`received-routes`** referencing **`table evpn.0`** validates **`route_bucket`** tagging—SONiC **`vtysh`** JSON snapshots reconcile **`sonic:show:bgp_evpn`** ingest cadence versus syslog jitter.
vSphere CMDB overlays tie **`peer_ip`** storms against clustered host migrations explaining benign symmetry—reject **`storm_flag`** positives lacking CLI corroboration across **`baseline_avg`** recomputation whenever collectors stall.
Cross-view **`cumulus:netq:bgp_evpn`** dashboards purely directional—Splunk macros remain authoritative when deltas diverge modestly.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard Studio workspace **`BGP EVPN Withdrawal Velocity`** stacks heat-map **`peer_ip`** × **`route_bucket`** colored by **`withdrawn_volume`**—timeline **`type2_withdrawals_per_min`** versus **`type5_withdrawals_per_min`** highlights **`storm_flag`** taxonomy—gauge lane renders **`ecmp_shrink_pct`** alongside textual **`ECMP set shrinkage`** cues sourced when **`ecmp_paths_before`** telemetry exists.
Alert routing directs **`esi_segment_burst`** storms toward Ethernet-segment SMEs—**`type5_storm`** rows toward WAN-facing architects—**`type3_anomaly`** toward multicast reviewers referencing **`imet_multicast_tree_rebuild`** overlays.
Embed Cisco **NDI** anomaly ribbons intersecting **`storm_ratio`** timelines via **`join`** **`splunk_server`** lookups referencing **`cisco:ndi:*`** feeds—tie pager narratives mentioning **`withdrawn`** bursts explicitly versus **`graceful_restart`** helper contexts flagged **`suppress`** macros.

Step 5 — Tuning and extensions
Cross-correlate prolonged **`withdrawn_volume`** tails against UC-18.3.4 **`VXLAN`** overlay narratives distinguishing dataplane **`VTEP`** loss storms versus pure withdrawal churn—never substitute **`overlay_fault`** macros blindly without verifying **`withdraw_hit`** causality.
Suppress **`storm_flag`** outputs referencing **`firmware_upgrade_window`** CMDB tags paired with **`ISSU`** sequencing plus **`MaintenanceWindow`** lookups guarding **`gr_helper_marker_walkthrough`** intervals honoring **`STALE`** retention timers documented inside BGP policies.
Feed **`mso_route_target_changeover`** **`CHANGE`** identifiers through **`route_target_changed`** booleans preventing **`type5_storm`** escalations tied purely to **`route-target`** filter choreography lacking dataplane symptoms.
Augment forensic drills joining UC-18.3.14 **`expected_bgp_peers`** lookups verifying **`peer_asn_seen`** aligns design **`ASN`** tuples whenever **`esi_segment_burst`** suspicion emerges alongside **`LLDP`** posture anomalies absent elsewhere.
Integrate Cisco **NDI** anomaly exports highlighting BGP-EVPN convergence categories—blend anomaly timestamps with **`withdrawn_volume`** spikes reinforcing pager narratives referencing **`fabric-control-plane-stability`** posture reviews quarterly.
Tune **`streamstats`** **`window`** sizes per fabric scale—dense multitenant estates may lengthen **`baseline_avg`** smoothing horizons preventing **`type2_sigma_burst`** chatter triggered nightly **`ARP`** rebalance chores unrelated materially toward faults elsewhere noted faithfully.
• **Silent alerts despite syslog spikes** — widen **`withdraw_hit`** regex families capturing **`BGP-5-WITHDRAW_BATCH`** multi-line syslog blobs—confirm **`props.conf`** **`LINE_BREAKER`** preserves **`WITHDRAW`** lines spanning newline boundaries.
• **`baseline_avg` stalls near zero** — scripted **`bgp:evpn:withdraw_event`** collectors paused—inspect **`splunkd.log`** **`ExecProcessor`** return codes alongside Ansible **`TASK`** banners referencing unreachable **`mgmt`** loops.
• **`ecmp_shrink_pct` always null** — collectors omit **`ecmp_paths_before`** fields—extend **`show`** transcripts harvesting **`nexthop`** breadth counts aligned per **`route`** hash groups documented vendor-side.
• **`storm_ratio` perpetual spikes** — **`window`** too narrow versus **`bin`** **`span=1m`** cadence—expand **`streamstats`** **`window`** toward **`45`** buckets smoothing intermittent bursts honoring **`withdraw_sigma_multiplier`** reviewer approvals quarterly archived faithfully.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for stretches when switches pull back lots of routing entries together so sharply that everyday programs stumble without warning long before anyone notices a loose cable or a flaky plug.
hardware: Multi-vendor BGP-EVPN spine-leaf fabrics: Cisco Nexus 9000 (NX-OS), Arista 7280 (EOS), Juniper QFX5120 (Junos), SONiC, Cumulus Linux switches with FRRouting bgpd.
